_doc: Source-verified dh_id corrections applied to the three extraction agents'
raw JSONL output for the Of Kings and Priests chunk (PR #218 — D&H Ch 4
sub-section 1, Dyn 21 Brief Lives, printed pp.205-209 / physical pp.190-194).
The OKP chunk uses the direct-PDF-Read method deviation (3 extraction
agents call `Read(file_path='raw/source-p190-p194.pdf', pages='1-5')`
instead of consuming an intermediate OCR'd `chunk-*.md` file produced by
a Gemini OCR subagent — see `prompt-ofkingsandpriests.md` § 'Method
deviation'). The Read tool's PDF renderer is deterministic, but the three
agents independently produced a small number of OCR-drift mismatches on
the `dh_id` field — specifically suffix-drop on Mutnodjmet B (B + C),
trailing-t-drop on Rudament(?) (C only), and J-prefix-drop / I-middle-drop
on the lacuna-ID cluster at the chunk tail (mixed across agents).
These would have raised the 3-agent merge's singleton-rejection on the
dh_id field (constitutional rule 2 — `_load_agent_chunks` requires every
row to appear in >=2 agents at the same composite key). The corrections
below resolve the OCR-drift to the source-verified canonical spelling.
Each correction was verified by direct main-session `Read` of the printed
source page, with the egyptologist-reviewer pass also confirming the
lacuna-ID J-prefix and It-middle readings against printed p.209.
This JSON is the committed audit trail. The actual application is
currently inline (the main agent rewrote each agent's `raw/agent-*-
ofkingsandpriests.jsonl` before re-running `merge.py`). Future direct-
PDF-Read chunks should build a `pre_merge.py` step that applies a
structured corrections file like this one automatically — tracked as a
follow-up to the dodson-hilton-queens source under issue #215. For
this chunk the gitignored agent JSONLs carry the post-correction state;
`reconciled.jsonl` is reproducible from this corrections file plus a
fresh agent re-run (correcting the drift back to canonical form).
Correction shape: each entry is keyed by '<agent_tag>|<wrong_dh_id>'.
Value is {'canonical_dh_id': str, 'rationale': str (citing source page)}.

b|Mutnodjmet.rationale: Agent B dropped the 'B' suffix on the headword. Printed p.207 (sub-PDF page 3) clearly prints 'Mutnodjmet B (KDB; KSis; KGW; L2L; 2PA Tanis)' as a single bold-italic headword with the disambiguating letter B. Agent A and the egyptologist-reviewer pre-fix sub-PDF read both confirm the suffix.

c|Mutnodjmet.rationale: Same as b|Mutnodjmet — agent C dropped the same suffix.

c|Rudamen(?).rationale: Agent C dropped the trailing 't' (printed source p.209 = sub-PDF page 5 reads 'Rudament(?)' with the question-mark hedge). Agents A and B both extracted 'Rudament(?)' correctly; 2/1 majority + source verification both favour 'Rudament(?)'.

b|[...]amenweret.rationale: Agent B dropped the J-prefix on the post-lacuna readable portion. Printed p.209 (sub-PDF page 5) right column clearly prints '[...]Jamenweret' with J-prefix; the egyptologist-reviewer pass against the printed page confirmed J on every entry in the lacuna cluster.

c|[...]amenweret.rationale: Same as b|[...]amenweret — agent C dropped the same J-prefix.

b|[...]anm.rationale: Agent B dropped the J-prefix on '[...]Janm' (KSonB). Verified against printed p.209.

c|[...]anm.rationale: Same as b|[...]anm — agent C dropped the same J-prefix.

b|[...]emkheb.rationale: Agent B dropped the J-prefix on '[...]Jemkheb' (KSonB). Verified against printed p.209.

c|[...]emkheb.rationale: Same as b|[...]emkheb — agent C dropped the same J-prefix.

a|[...]memsunay.rationale: All three agents dropped the J-prefix on '[...]Jmemsunay' (KSonB). The egyptologist-reviewer pass against printed p.209 surfaced the J-prefix on every entry in the lacuna cluster including this one; correction applied to all three agents for source fidelity.

b|[...]memsunay.rationale: Same as a|[...]memsunay — agent B dropped the same J-prefix.

c|[...]memsunay.rationale: Same as a|[...]memsunay — agent C dropped the same J-prefix.

a|[...]t[...] (KSonB).rationale: Agent A had two problems on this row: (1) dropped the 'I' from '[...]It[...]' (printed p.209 shows 'It' between the brackets) and (2) embedded the role parenthetical '(KSonB)' inside the dh_id string. Both corrected to source-verified canonical '[...]It[...]' (with 'I'); the role 'KSonB' remains correctly populated in the row's 'roles' field per the verbatim-role rule.

b|[...]t[...].rationale: Agent B dropped the 'I' from the middle of '[...]It[...]'. Verified against printed p.209.

c|[...]t[...].rationale: Same as b|[...]t[...] — agent C dropped the same 'I'.
